tools.name: add-record-to-list
tools.description: Add a record to a list as a new list entry. IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool: 1) You should call list-list-attribute-definitions for the target list to understand which entry attributes are available. 2) You should call list-records or search-records to find the record you want to add to the list. 3) You may call list-lists to find available lists. By default, duplicate entries are prevented - if the record already exists in the list, the existing entry ID is returned. Set allow_duplicates to true to create multiple entries for the same record.

list.description:
The ID or slug of the list to add the entry to.
format: slug or UUID
example: "sales_pipeline", "8a1cb283-1f38-467f-973a-c251e184b7ac"

parent_object.description:
The object type of the parent record being added to the list.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "people", "0a6aee89-2888-40cd-b165-ad6e1c493dd2"

parent_record_id.description:
The ID of the record to add to the list.
example: dff61db7-3f7a-46f4-b1f1-76f2419be618

entry_values.description:
Optional attribute values for the list entry. Use `api_slug` or `attribute_id` from `list-list-attribute-definitions` as the key.

# VALUE FORMATS BY ATTRIBUTE TYPE:

* text: String - "name": "Acme Corp"
* number: Number - "employee_count": 100
* checkbox: Boolean - "is_active": true
* date: ISO 8601 date - "founded_date": "2024-01-15"
* timestamp: ISO 8601 datetime - "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
* rating: Number 0-5 - "priority": 3
* email_address: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "email_addresses": ["alice@example.com"]
* phone_number: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "phone_numbers": ["+14155551234"]
* domain: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "domains": ["example.com", "attio.com"]
* select: Option title or UUID - "industry": "Software"
* status: Status title or UUID - "stage": "In Progress"
* currency: Number (uses attribute's configured currency) - "deal_value": 10000
* location: Address string - "address": "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US"
  Or object - {"line_1": "1 Infinite Loop", "locality": "Cupertino", "region": "CA", "postcode": "95014", "country_code": "US", "latitude": "37.331741", "longitude": "-122.030333"}
* personal_name: "Lastname, Firstname" format (note: last name comes FIRST) - "contact_name": "Doe, John"
  Or object with ALL THREE required fields: [{"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "full_name": "John Doe"}]
* actor_reference: Workspace member by email or UUID - "owner": "user@example.com"
* interaction: NOT WRITABLE - this attribute type cannot be set via the API

## RECORD REFERENCES (linking to other records):

Record reference attributes link to records in other objects (e.g., linking a deal to a company).

Formats supported:

1. By record ID: {"target_object": "companies", "target_record_id": "<uuid>"}
2. By matching attribute (must contain exactly ONE matching value):
   {"target_object": "people", "email_addresses": [{"email_address": "alice@example.com"}]}
   The key (e.g. "email_addresses") is an attribute slug on the target object.
3. Simple format (only if attribute references a single object type):
   - For people: pass an email address - "contact": "alice@example.com" (matches by email)
   - For companies: pass a domain - "company": "acme.com" (matches by domain)
   - Otherwise: pass a record UUID - "related_record": "<uuid>"

## ARRAYS VS SINGLE VALUES:

For attributes with is_multiselect=true, always provide an array.
For single-value attributes, you can use either format: "name": "value" or "name": ["value"]

allow_duplicates.description: Whether to allow adding a record that already exists in the list. Defaults to false, which prevents duplicate entries and returns an error with the existing entry ID instead.

tools.name: create-comment
tools.description: Creates a new comment on a record, list entry, or as a reply to an existing comment thread. Provide exactly one of: (parent_object + parent_record_id) for records, (parent_list + parent_entry_id) for list entries, or (parent_comment_id) for replies. Email addresses in the content will be automatically converted to @mentions if they match workspace members.

parent_object.description:
Create comment on a record. The object the record belongs to. Must be provided with parent_record_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "8dcc92ef-b6c3-45e9-bd7e-a3dd52976200"

parent_record_id.description:
Create comment on a record. The record ID. Must be provided with parent_object.
example: 7cb310f0-70ca-4f33-8b22-07d6ce5142e9

parent_list.description:
Create comment on a list entry. The list the entry belongs to. Must be provided with parent_entry_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "my_list", "0649076b-a586-487b-8567-0190cac69e98"

parent_entry_id.description:
Create comment on a list entry. The entry ID. Must be provided with parent_list.
example: a63ff71a-6051-4872-aabf-c1d36e2672c7

parent_comment_id.description:
Reply to an existing comment thread. The ID of the parent comment. Cannot be a reply - must be a top-level comment.
example: 260d7c1d-2587-4e10-bc94-1673535d3da0

content.description:
The comment content as plain text. Email addresses in the text will be automatically converted to @mentions if they match workspace members.
example: Great work on this deal! cc team@example.com

tools.name: create-note
tools.description: Creates a new note attached to a record. Returns the created note's ID.

title.description:
The title of the note.
example: Q1 Planning Meeting Notes

content.description:
The body of the note in markdown format.
format: Markdown. Supports: headings (#, ##, ###), lists (-, *, 1.), bold (**), italic (*), strikethrough (~~), highlight (==), links ([text](url)). Code blocks, images, and tables are not supported.
example: ## Summary\n\nDiscussed **roadmap** priorities.

meeting_id.description:
Optional ID to link to a specific meeting.
example: 7128e329-04f3-4bc0-a3a5-ccb6cbf26b3a

parent_object.description:
The object type for the parent record.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "509be798-6403-4866-9cf2-78463e9251c6"

parent_record_id.description:
The record ID to attach the note to.
example: 7255d60f-91f7-48ff-85fd-b21abed9e4d4

tools.name: create-record
tools.description: Create a new record in a given object. IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool, you should call list-attribute-definitions for the target object to understand which attributes are available, their types, whether they are required, and (for select/status fields) the valid options. Different attribute types require different value formats - see the 'values' parameter description for details.

object.description:
The ID or slug of the object to create the record in.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

values.description:
An object mapping attribute slugs or IDs to values. Use `api_slug` or `attribute_id` from `list-attribute-definitions` as the key.

# VALUE FORMATS BY ATTRIBUTE TYPE:

* text: String - "name": "Acme Corp"
* number: Number - "employee_count": 100
* checkbox: Boolean - "is_active": true
* date: ISO 8601 date - "founded_date": "2024-01-15"
* timestamp: ISO 8601 datetime - "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
* rating: Number 0-5 - "priority": 3
* email_address: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "email_addresses": ["alice@example.com"]
* phone_number: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "phone_numbers": ["+14155551234"]
* domain: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "domains": ["example.com", "attio.com"]
* select: Option title or UUID - "industry": "Software"
* status: Status title or UUID - "stage": "In Progress"
* currency: Number (uses attribute's configured currency) - "deal_value": 10000
* location: Address string - "address": "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US"
  Or object - {"line_1": "1 Infinite Loop", "locality": "Cupertino", "region": "CA", "postcode": "95014", "country_code": "US", "latitude": "37.331741", "longitude": "-122.030333"}
* personal_name: "Lastname, Firstname" format (note: last name comes FIRST) - "contact_name": "Doe, John"
  Or object with ALL THREE required fields: [{"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "full_name": "John Doe"}]
* actor_reference: Workspace member by email or UUID - "owner": "user@example.com"
* interaction: NOT WRITABLE - this attribute type cannot be set via the API

## RECORD REFERENCES (linking to other records):

Record reference attributes link to records in other objects (e.g., linking a deal to a company).

Formats supported:

1. By record ID: {"target_object": "companies", "target_record_id": "<uuid>"}
2. By matching attribute (must contain exactly ONE matching value):
   {"target_object": "people", "email_addresses": [{"email_address": "alice@example.com"}]}
   The key (e.g. "email_addresses") is an attribute slug on the target object.
3. Simple format (only if attribute references a single object type):
   - For people: pass an email address - "contact": "alice@example.com" (matches by email)
   - For companies: pass a domain - "company": "acme.com" (matches by domain)
   - Otherwise: pass a record UUID - "related_record": "<uuid>"

## ARRAYS VS SINGLE VALUES:

For attributes with is_multiselect=true, always provide an array.
For single-value attributes, you can use either format: "name": "value" or "name": ["value"]
example: {"actor_reference_by_id":"8b8da568-2e09-44ce-8463-c88bbf8b4106","actor_reference_by_email":"user@example.com","checkbox":true,"currency":13.37,"date":"2012-12-12","domain":["example.com","sub.example.com"],"email_address":["hello@example.com"],"location_simple_format":"1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US","location_object_format":{"line_1":"1 Infinite Loop","locality":"Cupertino","region":"CA","postcode":"95014","country_code":"US","latitude":"37.331741","longitude":"-122.030333"},"number":1337,"personal_name_simple_format":"Smith, John","personal_name_object_format":{"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","full_name":"John Owen Smith"},"rating":3,"rating_min":0,"rating_max":5,"record_reference_by_id":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"d79da64b-9717-4393-86a9-8c3485ff129e"}],"record_reference_by_value":[{"target_object":"people","email_addresses":[{"email_address":"test@example.com"}]}],"select":"Select option 1","select_multiselect":["Select option 1","Select option 2"],"text":"Text value","timestamp":"2012-12-12T12:12:121Z","b3d75773-871d-4aa0-92e6-757f2ff99274":"Example text value attribute referenced by ID","name":"Acme Corp","industry":"Software","employee_count":100,"primary_contact":"Doe, John","email_addresses":["contact@acme.com"],"associated_company":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"46cc9100-e0f0-4ca9-ac14-138cfe38de67"}]}

tools.name: create-task
tools.description: Creates a new task in Attio. Tasks can optionally have a deadline, an assignee (workspace member), and be linked to a record (e.g., a person or company). To assign to a workspace member, provide their workspace_member_id. To link to a record, provide both the object type (e.g., "people", "companies") and the record_id together. Returns the created task's ID.

content.description:
The text content of the task. MUST be provided.
example: Follow up on current software solutions

deadline_at.description:
The deadline date for the task.
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-02-15T00:00:00.000Z

is_completed.description:
Whether the task is completed. Defaults to false.
example: false

assignee_workspace_member_id.description:
The workspace member ID to assign the task to. Only one assignee can be specified via MCP.
example: 31abd9d6-667f-4240-b995-717be5ad126e

linked_record_object.description:
The object type for the linked record. Must be provided with linked_record_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies"

linked_record_id.description:
The record ID to link the task to. Must be provided with linked_record_object.
example: 492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f

tools.name: delete-comment
tools.description: Deletes a comment. You can only delete comments that you created. Deleting a parent comment will also delete all of its replies.

comment_id.description:
The ID of the comment to delete.
example: f30c2a19-fe96-493f-97a0-7a8a476dcc78

tools.name: get-call-recording
tools.description: Retrieves the full details of a call recording by ID, including its status, timestamps, and complete transcript.

meeting_id.description:
The ID of the meeting that the call recording is associated with.
example: a094d4ec-90bf-41f2-bbbd-f97043ca86c2

call_recording_id.description:
The ID of the call recording to retrieve.
example: 9e2e08b4-ae62-4afa-8093-13087b1567c5

tools.name: get-email-content
tools.description: Retrieves the full content/body of an email. Requires the mailbox_id and email_header_id, which can be obtained from email search results.

mailbox_id.description:
The ID of the mailbox containing the email.
example: e6bb3512-124b-4553-a393-faa0db15c7e6

email_id.description:
The ID of the email to retrieve content for.
example: 0ecabf53-6b13-4cf3-96cf-22416546d279

tools.name: get-note-body
tools.description: Get the body of a note by its ID

note_id.description:
The ID of the note to get the body of.
example: 1f06d2aa-bc46-4415-a030-b5ec054bd1e3

tools.name: get-records-by-ids
tools.description: Retrieve a given set of records by their IDs for a given object. Returns an array of records with their attribute values. If a record is not found, it will not be included in the response.

object.description:
The ID or slug of the object for which to retrieve records.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

record_ids.description:
The IDs of the records to retrieve. Records that are not found will not be included in the response.
example: ["ad27ac5f-95ab-4cc6-8880-e2655a1aae69", "d2e13173-1b77-48b3-ab1e-cf508f43c73e"]

tools.name: list-attribute-definitions
tools.description: List attribute definitions for a given object

object.description:
The ID or slug of the object for which to retrieve attribute definitions.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

include_archived.description: If true, the response will include archived attributes.

query.description: Plain text search term for fuzzy matching attribute titles and descriptions. If not specified or null, returns all attributes.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

tools.name: list-comment-replies
tools.description: Lists replies to a comment. Provide the comment_id of a top-level comment to fetch its replies. Cannot be used with reply comments - only top-level comments that were created on records or list entries. All replies in a thread use the same parent comment ID.

comment_id.description:
The ID of the parent comment thread to list replies for. Must be a top-level comment, not a reply.
example: 34f2cade-c2a2-45f6-a7e1-b88253f33af4

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

limit.description: Maximum number of replies to return.

tools.name: list-comments
tools.description: Lists comments on a record or list entry. Returns paginated top-level comments with up to 5 replies each. If a thread has more replies, use "list-comment-replies" to fetch additional replies. Provide either (parent_object and parent_record_id) to list comments on a record, or (parent_list and parent_entry_id) to list comments on a list entry.

parent_object.description:
Filter to comments on a specific record. The object the record belongs to. Must be provided with parent_record_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "3c96f90d-fb94-4be7-a379-537dc97d76c1"

parent_record_id.description:
Filter to comments on a specific record. The record ID. Must be provided with parent_object.
example: e49fd0eb-81aa-4592-8a59-a6fb6476ceb2

parent_list.description:
Filter to comments on a specific list entry. The list the entry belongs to. Must be provided with parent_entry_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "my_list", "bab8bc82-9ed6-480e-91e6-3cf45bcfb69b"

parent_entry_id.description:
Filter to comments on a specific list entry. The entry ID. Must be provided with parent_list.
example: d22f4162-6a48-4021-80c8-d33588ee35cb

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

limit.description: Maximum number of top-level comments to return.

tools.name: list-list-attribute-definitions
tools.description: List attribute definitions for a given list

list.description:
The ID or slug of the list for which to retrieve attribute definitions.
format: slug or UUID
example: "sales_pipeline", "ed08d6ef-a3b8-4bfb-816f-6d3a69a307a2"

include_archived.description: If true, the response will include archived attributes.

query.description: Plain text search term for fuzzy matching attribute titles and descriptions. If not specified or null, returns all attributes.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

tools.name: list-lists
tools.description: List all lists in the Attio workspace. Returns list metadata including ID, name, API slug, and parent object types. Use the query parameter to filter by name or slug.

query.description:
Filter returned lists using fuzzy search against list name or API slug. If null or not provided, returns all lists.
example: "sales pipeline"

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

tools.name: list-records
tools.description: List records in a given object with optional filtering and sorting. Returns paginated results. Use this tool to retrieve records that match specific criteria. You can filter on attributes using comparison operators, combine filters with AND/OR logic, and sort results by attribute values. Before calling this tool, ensure you know the attribute slugs or IDs for any filters or sorts you want to apply. Use the list-attribute-definitions tool to discover available attributes for an object.

object.description:
The object to list records from.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "people", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

filter.description:
Filter criteria to apply. If null, returns all records. Filter syntax supports three types:
1. Single condition: {"attribute":"name", "op":"eq", "value":"Acme Corp"}
2. AND group (all must match): {"and": [{"attribute":"industry", "op":"eq", "value":"Technology"}, {"attribute":"employee_count", "op":"gte", "value":50}]}
3. OR group (any must match): {"or": [{"attribute":"stage", "op":"eq", "value":"Proposal"}, {"attribute":"stage", "op":"eq", "value":"Negotiation"}]}
Groups can be nested. Available operators by type:
- text: eq, neq, contains, not-contains, starts-with, ends-with
- number/currency/rating: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte
- date: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g. "2024-03-15")
- timestamp: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (full ISO 8601 with timezone preferred, e.g. "2024-03-15T14:30:00Z". Bare dates default to midnight UTC)
- checkbox: eq, neq (boolean value)
- select/status: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (by option title or sort order)
- record_reference: eq, neq (use {object_id, record_id} value)
- actor_reference: eq, neq (workspace member UUID)
- domain/email/phone/personal_name: eq, neq, contains, not-contains, starts-with, ends-with
example: {"and": [{"attribute": "is_customer", "op": "eq", "value": true}, {"attribute": "employee_count", "op": "gte", "value": 50}]}

sorts.description:
Sorting to apply (max 2). If empty, results are returned in a deterministic random order. Sortable types: text, number, currency, date, timestamp, checkbox, rating, domain, email_address, phone_number, personal_name.
example: [{"attribute": "name", "direction": "asc"}]

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return (max 50).

offset.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

tools.name: list-records-in-list
tools.description: List entries in a given list with optional filtering and sorting. Returns paginated results. Use this tool to retrieve entries within a list that match specific criteria. You can filter on attributes using comparison operators, combine filters with AND/OR logic, and sort results by attribute values. Each entry includes the parent record it belongs to and all entry-level attributes.

list.description:
The list to query entries from.
format: slug or UUID
example: "my-sales-pipeline", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

filter.description:
Filter criteria to apply. If null, returns all entries. Filter syntax supports three types:
1. Single condition: {"attribute":"name", "op":"eq", "value":"Acme Corp"}
2. AND group (all must match): {"and": [{"attribute":"industry", "op":"eq", "value":"Technology"}, {"attribute":"employee_count", "op":"gte", "value":50}]}
3. OR group (any must match): {"or": [{"attribute":"stage", "op":"eq", "value":"Proposal"}, {"attribute":"stage", "op":"eq", "value":"Negotiation"}]}
Groups can be nested. Available operators by type:
- text: eq, neq, contains, not-contains, starts-with, ends-with
- number/currency/rating: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte
- date: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g. "2024-03-15")
- timestamp: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (full ISO 8601 with timezone preferred, e.g. "2024-03-15T14:30:00Z". Bare dates default to midnight UTC)
- checkbox: eq, neq (boolean value)
- select/status: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (by option title or sort order)
- record_reference: eq, neq (use {object_id, record_id} value)
- actor_reference: eq, neq (workspace member UUID)
- domain/email/phone/personal_name: eq, neq, contains, not-contains, starts-with, ends-with
example: {"and": [{"attribute": "is_customer", "op": "eq", "value": true}, {"attribute": "employee_count", "op": "gte", "value": 50}]}

sorts.description:
Sorting to apply (max 2). If empty, results are returned in a deterministic random order. Sortable types: text, number, currency, date, timestamp, checkbox, rating, domain, email_address, phone_number, personal_name.
example: [{"attribute": "name", "direction": "asc"}]

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (max 50).

offset.description: Number of entries to skip for pagination.

tools.name: list-tasks
tools.description: Lists tasks in the workspace with optional filters. Returns paginated results with task content, completion status, assignees, linked records, and deadlines. Use to find tasks by assignee, completion status, linked record, or date ranges.

assignee_workspace_member_id.description:
Filter by assignee. Pass a workspace member ID to find tasks assigned to that member. Pass null to find unassigned tasks. Omit to return tasks regardless of assignee.
example: 2d6b480e-18e2-4285-932e-ffc65fa91690

is_completed.description: Filter by completion status. true returns only completed tasks, false returns only incomplete tasks. Omit to return all tasks.

linked_record_object.description:
Filter to tasks linked to a specific record. The object the linked record belongs to. Must be provided with linked_record_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "daeeab8b-8128-469a-881b-0278e5da2e3d"

linked_record_id.description:
Filter to tasks linked to a specific record. The record ID. Must be provided with linked_record_object.
example: 3e5a6559-d470-4a20-b6c6-07c4111c362c

created_at_gte.description:
Filter to tasks created at or after this timestamp (inclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

created_at_lte.description:
Filter to tasks created at or before this timestamp (inclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

deadline_at_gte.description:
Filter to tasks with deadline at or after this timestamp (inclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

deadline_at_lte.description:
Filter to tasks with deadline at or before this timestamp (inclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

sort_by.description: Attribute to sort results by. Defaults to deadline_at.

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: list-workspace-members
tools.description: List members in the Attio workspace. Returns a list of matching members with their ID, email address, name, access level, and team memberships. Use the query parameter to filter by name, email, or team.

query.description:
Filter returned members using fuzzy search against member name, email address, and teams they are a member of. If null or not provided, returns all members.
example: "John Smith"

include_suspended.description: If true, response will include suspended members.

tools.name: list-workspace-teams
tools.description: List teams in the workspace. Teams are groups of workspace members used primarily for permission management. Returns an array of teams, each with: workspace_team_id, name, description, is_archived, created_at, and members (array of {name, email}).

include_archived.description: If true, the response will include archived teams.

tools.name: run-basic-report
tools.description: Run an aggregate report on records in an object or entries in a list. Use this tool when you need totals, averages, minimums, maximums, or grouped breakdowns. How to use: 1. Pick a source object/list via slug or UUID. 2. Choose a metric: - {"type":"count"} for counting records/entries. - {"type":"attribute", ...} for aggregating a numeric attribute. 3. Optionally add filter to narrow rows before aggregation. 4. Optionally add up to two group_by entries. The first is the primary grouping (outer key), the second is secondary (inner key). 5. If required, use the list attribute tools to discover valid attribute slugs for filter, metric paths, and grouping paths. Expected response shape: - group_by = []: plain number string, e.g. "42" or "1034.5" - one group_by: TOON key/value map, e.g. enterprise: 12 smb: 35 - two group_by: nested TOON map, e.g. emea: won: 4 open: 7 na: won: 3

source.description:
The source dataset for the report. Must be a list or object.
Use a list/object slug or UUID. The first segment in any metric attribute_path or group_by path must reference this same source.
Use list-lists to find lists and list-attribute-definitions to discover source attributes.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "d4ff323e-49f3-48bb-b8ce-f83a13db4a09", "top_customers"

attribute_path.description:
Path to traverse to reach the numeric value to aggregate. Path is made up of [object/list, attribute] pairs.
Each pair is made up of the object/list where the attribute lives, followed by the attribute: [["people", "twitter_follower_count"]]
For multi-hop paths through record references, chain the segments: [["companies", "employees"], ["people", "twitter_follower_count"]]
You may also use list/object/attribute IDs instead of slugs e.g. [["170cfaad-2656-46f6-b37e-ece8dc47fd6e", "c472827d-9031-4b1e-9839-eac22890ce34"], ["3d8269b2-f279-4b0f-bd5b-16b01d4e1ddd", "c09caf06-7441-4446-81a3-953243505139"]]
The first segment MUST always be the source object/list.
Lists are lists of records and have a special attribute called "parent_record" that points to the record that is the parent of the list entry.
For polymorphic references (attributes that can point to multiple objects), the object in the next segment disambiguates which branch to follow.
Polymorphic record references:
[["deals","customer"],["companies","revenue"]]
[["deals","customer"],["people","lifetime_value"]]
Polymorphic lists:
[["opportunities","parent_record"],["companies","revenue"]]
[["opportunities","parent_record"],["people","lifetime_value"]]
format: Array of [object/list,attribute] tuples (slugs or UUIDs)

filter.description:
Filter criteria to apply on the source dataset. If null, returns all records/entries from the source.
Filters are applied before metric aggregation and grouping. Filter syntax supports three types:
1. Single condition: {"attribute":"name", "op":"eq", "value":"Acme Corp"}
2. AND group (all must match): {"and": [{"attribute":"industry", "op":"eq", "value":"Technology"}, {"attribute":"employee_count", "op":"gte", "value":50}]}
3. OR group (any must match): {"or": [{"attribute":"stage", "op":"eq", "value":"Proposal"}, {"attribute":"stage", "op":"eq", "value":"Negotiation"}]}
Groups can be nested. Available operators by type:
- text: eq, neq, contains, not-contains, starts-with, ends-with
- number/currency/rating: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte
- date: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g. "2024-03-15")
- timestamp: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (full ISO 8601 with timezone preferred, e.g. "2024-03-15T14:30:00Z". Bare dates default to midnight UTC)
- checkbox: eq, neq (boolean value)
- select/status: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte (by option title or sort order)
- record_reference: eq, neq (use {object_id, record_id} value)
- actor_reference: eq, neq (workspace member UUID)
- domain/email/phone/personal_name: eq, neq, contains, not-contains, starts-with, ends-with
example: {"and": [{"attribute": "is_customer", "op": "eq", "value": true}, {"attribute": "employee_count", "op": "gte", "value": 50}]}

path.description:
The path to group by. Path is made up of [object/list, attribute] pairs.
Each pair is made up of the object/list where the attribute lives, followed by the attribute: [["people", "twitter_follower_count"]]
For multi-hop paths through record references, chain the segments: [["companies", "employees"], ["people", "twitter_follower_count"]]
You may also use list/object/attribute IDs instead of slugs e.g. [["170cfaad-2656-46f6-b37e-ece8dc47fd6e", "c472827d-9031-4b1e-9839-eac22890ce34"], ["3d8269b2-f279-4b0f-bd5b-16b01d4e1ddd", "c09caf06-7441-4446-81a3-953243505139"]]
The first segment MUST always be the source object/list.
Lists are lists of records and have a special attribute called "parent_record" that points to the record that is the parent of the list entry.
For polymorphic references (attributes that can point to multiple objects), the object in the next segment disambiguates which branch to follow.
Polymorphic record references:
[["deals","customer"],["companies","revenue"]]
[["deals","customer"],["people","lifetime_value"]]
Polymorphic lists:
[["opportunities","parent_record"],["companies","revenue"]]
[["opportunities","parent_record"],["people","lifetime_value"]]
format: Array of [object/list,attribute] tuples (slugs or UUIDs)

field.description:
Which field on the final path attribute to group by. If not provided, the default field will be used.
The following attribute types are simple fields that do not need to specify a field:
- text
- number
- checkbox
- date
- timestamp
- rating
- status
- pipeline
- select
- record reference
- actor reference
The following fields are available for complex attribute types:
- email address:
  - normalized_email_address (default)
  - email_domain
- personal name:
  - full_name (default)
  - first_name
  - last_name
- location (no default; MUST specify a field):
  - line_1
  - line_2
  - line_3
  - line_4
  - locality
  - region
  - postcode
  - country_code
The following attribute types are not usable for grouping:
- interaction
            
format: string

time_domain.description:
The time domain to report on.
format: string

group_by.description:
One or more values to group by (max 2).
If omitted, the response is a single number.
If one grouping is provided, response is an object keyed by that grouping value.
If two groupings are provided, response is a nested object keyed by primary then secondary grouping value.
For two groupings, group_by[0] is primary (outer key) and group_by[1] is secondary (inner key).
Examples:
- [{"path":[["companies","industry"]]}]
- [{"path":[["deals","stage"]]},{"path":[["deals","owner"]]}]
format: Array of group by objects

tools.name: search-call-recordings-by-metadata
tools.description: Searches all call recordings across the workspace by their metadata. Useful for finding call recordings based on who was on the call (workspace members or person records), related records (e.g., companies or deals), meeting title, and time range. Returns paginated results ordered by start time (most recent first). Uses limit/offset pagination. Returns call recording metadata only (IDs and timestamps). Use the "get-call-recording" tool to retrieve the full transcript of a call recording.

speaker_workspace_member_ids.description:
Filter to call recordings where specific workspace members were speakers. All specified members must be present on the call.
example: ["033efe8c-76b6-42b0-9a52-d95de07b29db"]

speaker_person_record_ids.description:
Filter to call recordings where specific person records were speakers. All specified persons must be present on the call.
example: ["89e3da33-99ab-4289-bd67-f60a9a998758"]

related_record_object.description:
The object type of the related records. Must be provided with related_record_ids.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "46496735-657a-4636-a9de-06a66b70cdc8"

related_record_ids.description:
Filter to call recordings associated with specific records (e.g., companies or deals). Must be provided with related_record_object.
example: ["a1702de1-4017-4160-a3dd-ebc4d96482b3"]

meeting_title_query.description:
Search for call recordings by meeting title. Supports full-text search with query enhancement (synonyms, stemming) and substring matching for partial queries.
example: quarterly review

starts_after.description:
Filter to call recordings that started on or after this timestamp (inclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

starts_before.description:
Filter to call recordings that started on or before this timestamp (inclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

tools.name: search-emails-by-metadata
tools.description: Searches emails visible to the user by their metadata. Useful for finding emails based on participants, domain, and time range. Returns paginated results ordered by sent time (most recent first). Uses limit/offset pagination. When both domain and participant_email_addresses are provided, they are ORed together (emails matching either filter are returned). Returns email metadata only (ID, subject, summary, snippet, sender, sent_at). Use the "get-email-content" tool to retrieve the full content of an email.

participant_email_addresses.description:
Filter by email participants. Filters by participants of all roles (from, to, cc, bcc). ORed with domain filter if both are provided.
format: Array of valid email addresses.
example: ["john@example.com", "jane@example.com"]

domain.description:
Filter by domain the email came from or was sent to. ORed with participant_email_addresses filter if both are provided.
example: example.com

sent_at_gt.description:
Filter to emails sent after this timestamp.
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z

sent_at_lt.description:
Filter to emails sent before this timestamp.
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-31T23:59:59.999Z

limit.description:
Maximum number of results to return.
example: 10

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

tools.name: search-meetings
tools.description: Search across past and future meetings in the workspace. Returns paginated results with meeting metadata and associated call recording IDs. Useful for finding meetings based on participants, related records, and time range. Results are split into past_meetings (sorted by start time descending) and future_meetings (sorted by start time ascending). Returns call_recording_ids for past meetings so you can fetch full transcripts via the "get-call-recording" tool.

participant_email_addresses.description:
Filter to meetings where participants with matching email addresses are present. Uses normalized email addresses (case-insensitive).
example: ["alice@example.com", "bob@example.com"]

participant_email_addresses_operator.description:
AND = all specified emails must be participants. OR = any of the specified emails is a participant. Only valid when participant_email_addresses is provided.
example: "OR"

related_record_object.description:
The object type for related record filtering. Must be provided with related_record_ids.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "deals", "69d22575-c85e-4f20-9158-8dcdefbc5835"

related_record_ids.description:
Filter to meetings linked to specific records. Must be provided with related_record_object.
example: ["90394f27-349a-4d4e-827c-8e2a9ed86c59"]

related_records_operator.description:
AND = meeting must be linked to ALL specified records. OR = meeting must be linked to ANY of the specified records.
example: "AND"

starts_after.description:
Filter to meetings starting after this timestamp (exclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z

starts_before.description:
Filter to meetings starting before this timestamp (exclusive).
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-31T23:59:59.999Z

timezone.description:
IANA timezone string for all-day meeting calculations. If an invalid timezone is provided, UTC will be used as a fallback.
example: "America/New_York", "Europe/London"

tools.name: search-notes-by-metadata
tools.description: Searches for notes by their metadata. Useful for finding notes based on parent record, meeting, author (workspace member), and creation time. Returns paginated results ordered by creation date (most recent first). Uses limit/offset pagination.

parent_record_object.description:
Use to filter to notes attached to a specific record. Must be provided with parent_record_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

parent_record_id.description:
Use to filter to notes attached to a specific record. Must be provided with parent_record_object.
example: "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

meeting_id.description:
Use to filter to notes associated with a specific meeting.
example: d2e13173-1b77-48b3-ab1e-cf508f43c73e

workspace_membership_id.description:
Use to filter to notes associated with a specific workspace member.
example: 31abd9d6-667f-4240-b995-717be5ad126e

created_at_gt.description:
Use to filter to notes created after a specific point in timestamp.
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

created_at_lt.description:
Use to filter to notes created before a specific point in timestamp.
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-01-20T00:00:00.000Z

tools.name: search-records
tools.description: Perform a full-text search for records in a given object. Only searches indexed attributes: domains, email addresses, phone numbers, name/title, description, social handles, and location. Returns paginated results.

object.description:
The ID or slug of the object to search records in.
format: slug or UUID
example: "companies", "492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f"

query.description:
Case-insensitive, full-text search query. Searches indexed attributes: domains, email addresses, phone numbers, name/title, description, social handles, and location.
example: "Acme Corp"

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

tools.name: semantic-search-call-recordings
tools.description: Searches all call recordings across the workspace using semantic similarity. Returns call recordings that are most semantically similar to the query, even if the exact keywords are not present in the transcript. Useful for finding calls where a specific topic was discussed. Searches both transcript content and call recording overviews (title and summary) automatically. Returns matching transcript chunks and call recording overview information. Use the "get-call-recording" tool to retrieve the full transcript of a call recording.

query.description:
The search query to find semantically similar call recordings. Searches both transcript content and call recording overviews (title and summary) using vector embeddings, finding calls where similar topics were discussed even if the exact keywords are not present.
example: quarterly revenue discussion

tools.name: semantic-search-emails
tools.description: Searches emails visible to the user using semantic similarity. Returns up to 20 emails that are most semantically similar to the query, even if exact keywords are not present. Useful for finding emails where a specific topic was discussed. Returns email metadata only (ID, subject, summary, snippet, sender, sent_at). Use the "get-email-content" tool to retrieve the full content of an email.

query.description:
The search query for semantic/content-based search.
example: meeting notes from last quarter

include_emails_of_others.description:
If true, search through emails of other workspace members as well. If false, only search through the user's own mailbox.
example: false

tools.name: semantic-search-notes
tools.description: Searches all notes across the workspace using semantic similarity. Returns up to 20 notes that are most semantically similar to the query, even if the exact keywords are not present in the note. Useful for finding notes where a specific topic was discussed. Returns note metadata only (ID, title, timestamps, parent record). Use the "get-note-body" tool to retrieve the full content of a note.

query.description:
The search query to find semantically similar notes. This searches the body content of notes using vector embeddings, finding notes where similar topics were discussed even if the exact keywords are not present.
example: quarterly revenue discussion

tools.name: update-list
tools.description: Update a list's name or API slug

list.description:
The ID or slug of the list to update.
format: slug or UUID
example: "sales_pipeline", "ddc9d72e-ecf6-4f8b-ab49-67486016027e"

name.description:
The new human-readable name of the list.
example: "Enterprise Sales"

api_slug.description:
The new API slug for the list. Must be formatted in snake_case.
example: "enterprise_sales"

tools.name: update-list-entry-by-id
tools.description: Update attribute values on an existing list entry by its entry ID. Before calling this tool, you must: - Call list-records-in-list to find the entry you want to update. You may also: - Call list-list-attribute-definitions for the target list to understand which entry attributes are available. - Call list-lists to find available lists. Note: The parent record cannot be changed after creation.

list.description:
The ID or slug of the list containing the entry.
format: slug or UUID
example: "sales_pipeline", "f3cf5bbf-4cd4-43db-91e8-2e30d86c3596"

entry_id.description:
The ID of the list entry to update.
example: 028c0944-7747-4a7d-88d5-c5c9a971cb35

entry_values.description:
An object mapping attribute slugs or IDs to values. Use `api_slug` or `attribute_id` from `list-list-attribute-definitions` as the key.

# VALUE FORMATS BY ATTRIBUTE TYPE:

* text: String - "name": "Acme Corp"
* number: Number - "employee_count": 100
* checkbox: Boolean - "is_active": true
* date: ISO 8601 date - "founded_date": "2024-01-15"
* timestamp: ISO 8601 datetime - "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
* rating: Number 0-5 - "priority": 3
* email_address: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "email_addresses": ["alice@example.com"]
* phone_number: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "phone_numbers": ["+14155551234"]
* domain: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "domains": ["example.com", "attio.com"]
* select: Option title or UUID - "industry": "Software"
* status: Status title or UUID - "stage": "In Progress"
* currency: Number (uses attribute's configured currency) - "deal_value": 10000
* location: Address string - "address": "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US"
  Or object - {"line_1": "1 Infinite Loop", "locality": "Cupertino", "region": "CA", "postcode": "95014", "country_code": "US", "latitude": "37.331741", "longitude": "-122.030333"}
* personal_name: "Lastname, Firstname" format (note: last name comes FIRST) - "contact_name": "Doe, John"
  Or object with ALL THREE required fields: [{"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "full_name": "John Doe"}]
* actor_reference: Workspace member by email or UUID - "owner": "user@example.com"
* interaction: NOT WRITABLE - this attribute type cannot be set via the API

## RECORD REFERENCES (linking to other records):

Record reference attributes link to records in other objects (e.g., linking a deal to a company).

Formats supported:

1. By record ID: {"target_object": "companies", "target_record_id": "<uuid>"}
2. By matching attribute (must contain exactly ONE matching value):
   {"target_object": "people", "email_addresses": [{"email_address": "alice@example.com"}]}
   The key (e.g. "email_addresses") is an attribute slug on the target object.
3. Simple format (only if attribute references a single object type):
   - For people: pass an email address - "contact": "alice@example.com" (matches by email)
   - For companies: pass a domain - "company": "acme.com" (matches by domain)
   - Otherwise: pass a record UUID - "related_record": "<uuid>"

## ARRAYS VS SINGLE VALUES:

For attributes with is_multiselect=true, always provide an array.
For single-value attributes, you can use either format: "name": "value" or "name": ["value"]
example: {"actor_reference_by_id":"8b8da568-2e09-44ce-8463-c88bbf8b4106","actor_reference_by_email":"user@example.com","checkbox":true,"currency":13.37,"date":"2012-12-12","domain":["example.com","sub.example.com"],"email_address":["hello@example.com"],"location_simple_format":"1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US","location_object_format":{"line_1":"1 Infinite Loop","locality":"Cupertino","region":"CA","postcode":"95014","country_code":"US","latitude":"37.331741","longitude":"-122.030333"},"number":1337,"personal_name_simple_format":"Smith, John","personal_name_object_format":{"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","full_name":"John Owen Smith"},"rating":3,"rating_min":0,"rating_max":5,"record_reference_by_id":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"d79da64b-9717-4393-86a9-8c3485ff129e"}],"record_reference_by_value":[{"target_object":"people","email_addresses":[{"email_address":"test@example.com"}]}],"select":"Select option 1","select_multiselect":["Select option 1","Select option 2"],"text":"Text value","timestamp":"2012-12-12T12:12:121Z","b3d75773-871d-4aa0-92e6-757f2ff99274":"Example text value attribute referenced by ID","name":"Acme Corp","industry":"Software","employee_count":100,"primary_contact":"Doe, John","email_addresses":["contact@acme.com"],"associated_company":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"46cc9100-e0f0-4ca9-ac14-138cfe38de67"}]}

patch_multiselect_values.description: If `true`, values provided for multiselect attributes will be created and prepended to the list of values that already exist (if any). If `false`, values provided for multiselect attributes will be either created or deleted as necessary to match the list of supplied values. Defaults to `true`.

tools.name: update-list-entry-by-record-id
tools.description: Update attribute values on a list entry by finding it via its parent record ID. IMPORTANT: This tool will error if the record has zero or multiple entries in the specified list. Before calling this tool, you may: 1) Call list-lists to find available lists. 2) Call list-list-attribute-definitions for the target list to understand which entry attributes are available. 3) Call list-records or search-records to find the record ID.

list.description:
The ID or slug of the list containing the entry.
format: slug or UUID
example: "sales_pipeline", "2e792761-665b-4d9e-98ae-ea84894f8a26"

parent_object.description:
The slug or ID of the object that the parent record belongs to (e.g. 'people' or 'companies').
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "2337d5ad-c1ee-4dd2-ae43-56bdbcc9b878"

parent_record_id.description:
The ID of the parent record. The tool will find the list entry associated with this record.
example: 7f08724c-4e3f-4fab-9b1e-5a62174a95f1

entry_values.description:
An object mapping attribute slugs or IDs to values. Use `api_slug` or `attribute_id` from `list-list-attribute-definitions` as the key.

# VALUE FORMATS BY ATTRIBUTE TYPE:

* text: String - "name": "Acme Corp"
* number: Number - "employee_count": 100
* checkbox: Boolean - "is_active": true
* date: ISO 8601 date - "founded_date": "2024-01-15"
* timestamp: ISO 8601 datetime - "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
* rating: Number 0-5 - "priority": 3
* email_address: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "email_addresses": ["alice@example.com"]
* phone_number: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "phone_numbers": ["+14155551234"]
* domain: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "domains": ["example.com", "attio.com"]
* select: Option title or UUID - "industry": "Software"
* status: Status title or UUID - "stage": "In Progress"
* currency: Number (uses attribute's configured currency) - "deal_value": 10000
* location: Address string - "address": "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US"
  Or object - {"line_1": "1 Infinite Loop", "locality": "Cupertino", "region": "CA", "postcode": "95014", "country_code": "US", "latitude": "37.331741", "longitude": "-122.030333"}
* personal_name: "Lastname, Firstname" format (note: last name comes FIRST) - "contact_name": "Doe, John"
  Or object with ALL THREE required fields: [{"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "full_name": "John Doe"}]
* actor_reference: Workspace member by email or UUID - "owner": "user@example.com"
* interaction: NOT WRITABLE - this attribute type cannot be set via the API

## RECORD REFERENCES (linking to other records):

Record reference attributes link to records in other objects (e.g., linking a deal to a company).

Formats supported:

1. By record ID: {"target_object": "companies", "target_record_id": "<uuid>"}
2. By matching attribute (must contain exactly ONE matching value):
   {"target_object": "people", "email_addresses": [{"email_address": "alice@example.com"}]}
   The key (e.g. "email_addresses") is an attribute slug on the target object.
3. Simple format (only if attribute references a single object type):
   - For people: pass an email address - "contact": "alice@example.com" (matches by email)
   - For companies: pass a domain - "company": "acme.com" (matches by domain)
   - Otherwise: pass a record UUID - "related_record": "<uuid>"

## ARRAYS VS SINGLE VALUES:

For attributes with is_multiselect=true, always provide an array.
For single-value attributes, you can use either format: "name": "value" or "name": ["value"]
example: {"actor_reference_by_id":"8b8da568-2e09-44ce-8463-c88bbf8b4106","actor_reference_by_email":"user@example.com","checkbox":true,"currency":13.37,"date":"2012-12-12","domain":["example.com","sub.example.com"],"email_address":["hello@example.com"],"location_simple_format":"1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US","location_object_format":{"line_1":"1 Infinite Loop","locality":"Cupertino","region":"CA","postcode":"95014","country_code":"US","latitude":"37.331741","longitude":"-122.030333"},"number":1337,"personal_name_simple_format":"Smith, John","personal_name_object_format":{"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","full_name":"John Owen Smith"},"rating":3,"rating_min":0,"rating_max":5,"record_reference_by_id":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"d79da64b-9717-4393-86a9-8c3485ff129e"}],"record_reference_by_value":[{"target_object":"people","email_addresses":[{"email_address":"test@example.com"}]}],"select":"Select option 1","select_multiselect":["Select option 1","Select option 2"],"text":"Text value","timestamp":"2012-12-12T12:12:121Z","b3d75773-871d-4aa0-92e6-757f2ff99274":"Example text value attribute referenced by ID","name":"Acme Corp","industry":"Software","employee_count":100,"primary_contact":"Doe, John","email_addresses":["contact@acme.com"],"associated_company":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"46cc9100-e0f0-4ca9-ac14-138cfe38de67"}]}

patch_multiselect_values.description: If `true`, values provided for multiselect attributes will be created and prepended to the list of values that already exist (if any). If `false`, values provided for multiselect attributes will be either created or deleted as necessary to match the list of supplied values. Defaults to `true`.

tools.name: update-note
tools.description: Appends or prepends plain-text content to an existing note and/or updates its title. At least one of `operation` or `updated_title` must be provided.

note_id.description:
The ID of the note to update.
example: 8ed67289-0630-444d-815c-fbb3d670b30d

operation.description: Optional content update. `append` adds paragraphs to the end of the note, `prepend` adds paragraphs to the start. `content` is plain text; each newline starts a new paragraph.

updated_title.description:
Optional new title for the note.
example: Q1 Planning Meeting Notes

tools.name: update-record
tools.description: Use this tool to update people, companies, and other records by `record_id`.

object.description:
The object type for the target record.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "98dad68d-3183-4828-9074-5bd0163f2d7f"

record_id.description:
A UUID of the record to update.
example: e20d6297-a6de-4d83-bfa7-9c6246084599

values.description:
An object mapping attribute slugs or IDs to values. Use `api_slug` or `attribute_id` from `list-attribute-definitions` as the key.

# VALUE FORMATS BY ATTRIBUTE TYPE:

* text: String - "name": "Acme Corp"
* number: Number - "employee_count": 100
* checkbox: Boolean - "is_active": true
* date: ISO 8601 date - "founded_date": "2024-01-15"
* timestamp: ISO 8601 datetime - "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
* rating: Number 0-5 - "priority": 3
* email_address: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "email_addresses": ["alice@example.com"]
* phone_number: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "phone_numbers": ["+14155551234"]
* domain: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "domains": ["example.com", "attio.com"]
* select: Option title or UUID - "industry": "Software"
* status: Status title or UUID - "stage": "In Progress"
* currency: Number (uses attribute's configured currency) - "deal_value": 10000
* location: Address string - "address": "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US"
  Or object - {"line_1": "1 Infinite Loop", "locality": "Cupertino", "region": "CA", "postcode": "95014", "country_code": "US", "latitude": "37.331741", "longitude": "-122.030333"}
* personal_name: "Lastname, Firstname" format (note: last name comes FIRST) - "contact_name": "Doe, John"
  Or object with ALL THREE required fields: [{"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "full_name": "John Doe"}]
* actor_reference: Workspace member by email or UUID - "owner": "user@example.com"
* interaction: NOT WRITABLE - this attribute type cannot be set via the API

## RECORD REFERENCES (linking to other records):

Record reference attributes link to records in other objects (e.g., linking a deal to a company).

Formats supported:

1. By record ID: {"target_object": "companies", "target_record_id": "<uuid>"}
2. By matching attribute (must contain exactly ONE matching value):
   {"target_object": "people", "email_addresses": [{"email_address": "alice@example.com"}]}
   The key (e.g. "email_addresses") is an attribute slug on the target object.
3. Simple format (only if attribute references a single object type):
   - For people: pass an email address - "contact": "alice@example.com" (matches by email)
   - For companies: pass a domain - "company": "acme.com" (matches by domain)
   - Otherwise: pass a record UUID - "related_record": "<uuid>"

## ARRAYS VS SINGLE VALUES:

For attributes with is_multiselect=true, always provide an array.
For single-value attributes, you can use either format: "name": "value" or "name": ["value"]
example: {"actor_reference_by_id":"8b8da568-2e09-44ce-8463-c88bbf8b4106","actor_reference_by_email":"user@example.com","checkbox":true,"currency":13.37,"date":"2012-12-12","domain":["example.com","sub.example.com"],"email_address":["hello@example.com"],"location_simple_format":"1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US","location_object_format":{"line_1":"1 Infinite Loop","locality":"Cupertino","region":"CA","postcode":"95014","country_code":"US","latitude":"37.331741","longitude":"-122.030333"},"number":1337,"personal_name_simple_format":"Smith, John","personal_name_object_format":{"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","full_name":"John Owen Smith"},"rating":3,"rating_min":0,"rating_max":5,"record_reference_by_id":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"d79da64b-9717-4393-86a9-8c3485ff129e"}],"record_reference_by_value":[{"target_object":"people","email_addresses":[{"email_address":"test@example.com"}]}],"select":"Select option 1","select_multiselect":["Select option 1","Select option 2"],"text":"Text value","timestamp":"2012-12-12T12:12:121Z","b3d75773-871d-4aa0-92e6-757f2ff99274":"Example text value attribute referenced by ID","name":"Acme Corp","industry":"Software","employee_count":100,"primary_contact":"Doe, John","email_addresses":["contact@acme.com"],"associated_company":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"46cc9100-e0f0-4ca9-ac14-138cfe38de67"}]}

patch_multiselect_values.description: If `true`, values provided for multiselect attributes will be created and prepended to the list of values that already exist (if any). If `false`, values provied for multiselect attributes will be either created or deleted as necessary to match the list of supplied values. Defaults to `true`.

tools.name: update-task
tools.description: Updates an existing task in Attio. You can update the deadline, completion status, assignee, and linked record. To update the assignee, provide their workspace_member_id. To update the linked record, provide both the object type (e.g., "people", "companies") and the record_id together. To clear the deadline, set deadline_at to null. Returns the updated task's ID.

task_id.description:
The ID of the task to update.
example: 492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f

deadline_at.description:
The deadline date for the task. Set to null to clear the deadline.
format: ISO 8601 timestamp string. UTC timezone.
example: 2026-02-15T00:00:00.000Z

is_completed.description:
Whether the task is completed.
example: true

assignee_workspace_member_id.description:
The workspace member ID to assign the task to. Only one assignee can be specified via MCP.
example: 31abd9d6-667f-4240-b995-717be5ad126e

linked_record_object.description:
The object type for the linked record. Must be provided with linked_record_id.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies"

linked_record_id.description:
The record ID to link the task to. Must be provided with linked_record_object.
example: 492c42ea-4def-4e51-9d1b-9ce24fc9e57f

tools.name: upsert-record
tools.description: Use this tool to create or update people, companies and other records. A matching attribute is used to search for existing records. If a record is found with the same value for the `matching_attribute`, that record will be updated. If no record with the same value for the `matching_attribute` is found, a new record will be created instead. If the matching attribute is a multiselect attribute, new values will be added and existing values will not be deleted. If you would like to avoid matching, please use the `create-record` tool.

matching_attribute.description:
The ID or slug of the attribute to use to check if a record already exists. The attribute must be unique. The value for `matching_attribute` MUST be provided in the `values` payload.
example: "email_addresses", "domains", "874ab3ef-8e12-414a-8018-1356e9ca07fd"

object.description:
The object type for the target record.
format: slug or UUID
example: "people", "companies", "ab2ee16b-b11b-4dd0-b7ca-d2aaa77210f6"

patch_multiselect_values.description: If `true`, values provided for multiselect attributes will be created and prepended to the list of values that already exist (if any). If `false`, values provied for multiselect attributes will be either created or deleted as necessary to match the list of supplied values. Defaults to `true`.

values.description:
An object mapping attribute slugs or IDs to values. Use `api_slug` or `attribute_id` from `list-attribute-definitions` as the key.

# VALUE FORMATS BY ATTRIBUTE TYPE:

* text: String - "name": "Acme Corp"
* number: Number - "employee_count": 100
* checkbox: Boolean - "is_active": true
* date: ISO 8601 date - "founded_date": "2024-01-15"
* timestamp: ISO 8601 datetime - "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
* rating: Number 0-5 - "priority": 3

* phone_number: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "phone_numbers": ["+14155551234"]
* domain: String or array of strings (depends on is_multiselect) - "domains": ["example.com", "attio.com"]
* select: Option title or UUID - "industry": "Software"
* status: Status title or UUID - "stage": "In Progress"
* currency: Number (uses attribute's configured currency) - "deal_value": 10000
* location: Address string - "address": "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US"
  Or object - {"line_1": "1 Infinite Loop", "locality": "Cupertino", "region": "CA", "postcode": "95014", "country_code": "US", "latitude": "37.331741", "longitude": "-122.030333"}
* personal_name: "Lastname, Firstname" format (note: last name comes FIRST) - "contact_name": "Doe, John"
  Or object with ALL THREE required fields: [{"first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "full_name": "John Doe"}]
* actor_reference: Workspace member by email or UUID - "owner": "user@example.com"
* interaction: NOT WRITABLE - this attribute type cannot be set via the API

## RECORD REFERENCES (linking to other records):

Record reference attributes link to records in other objects (e.g., linking a deal to a company).

Formats supported:

1. By record ID: {"target_object": "companies", "target_record_id": "<uuid>"}
2. By matching attribute (must contain exactly ONE matching value):
   {"target_object": "people", "email_addresses": [{"email_address": "alice@example.com"}]}
   The key (e.g. "email_addresses") is an attribute slug on the target object.
3. Simple format (only if attribute references a single object type):
   - For people: pass an email address - "contact": "alice@example.com" (matches by email)
   - For companies: pass a domain - "company": "acme.com" (matches by domain)
   - Otherwise: pass a record UUID - "related_record": "<uuid>"

## ARRAYS VS SINGLE VALUES:

For attributes with is_multiselect=true, always provide an array.
For single-value attributes, you can use either format: "name": "value" or "name": ["value"]
example: {"actor_reference_by_id":"8b8da568-2e09-44ce-8463-c88bbf8b4106","actor_reference_by_email":"user@example.com","checkbox":true,"currency":13.37,"date":"2012-12-12","domain":["example.com","sub.example.com"],"email_address":["hello@example.com"],"location_simple_format":"1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA, 95014, US","location_object_format":{"line_1":"1 Infinite Loop","locality":"Cupertino","region":"CA","postcode":"95014","country_code":"US","latitude":"37.331741","longitude":"-122.030333"},"number":1337,"personal_name_simple_format":"Smith, John","personal_name_object_format":{"first_name":"John","last_name":"Smith","full_name":"John Owen Smith"},"rating":3,"rating_min":0,"rating_max":5,"record_reference_by_id":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"d79da64b-9717-4393-86a9-8c3485ff129e"}],"record_reference_by_value":[{"target_object":"people","email_addresses":[{"email_address":"test@example.com"}]}],"select":"Select option 1","select_multiselect":["Select option 1","Select option 2"],"text":"Text value","timestamp":"2012-12-12T12:12:121Z","b3d75773-871d-4aa0-92e6-757f2ff99274":"Example text value attribute referenced by ID","name":"Acme Corp","industry":"Software","employee_count":100,"primary_contact":"Doe, John","email_addresses":["contact@acme.com"],"associated_company":[{"target_object":"companies","target_record_id":"46cc9100-e0f0-4ca9-ac14-138cfe38de67"}]}

tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Returns information about the current user's identity and workspace membership, including their email, name, workspace member ID, access level, and workspace name.

tools.name: create_view
tools.description: Renders a hand-drawn diagram using Excalidraw elements. Elements stream in one by one with draw-on animations. Call read_me first to learn the element format.

elements.description: JSON array string of Excalidraw elements. Must be valid JSON — no comments, no trailing commas. Keep compact. Call read_me first for format reference.

tools.name: read_me
tools.description: Returns the Excalidraw element format reference with color palettes, examples, and tips. Call this BEFORE using create_view for the first time.
